title: Dracaena borneensis

intro: Dracaena borneensis, also known as the Bornean dragon tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asparagaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Dracaena borneensis include dracaenol, borneol, and other sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, coughs, and skin irritations.
